Hey Yaji! Final year’s a beast—half the time I feel like I’m coding my way out of a cave, but it’s oddly satisfying. I’m working on a small indie game project, basically a 2D platformer with procedurally generated levels. The twist? I’m using a lightweight reinforcement learning model to tweak the difficulty in real-time based on the player’s performance. It’s a bit of a wild experiment—trying to balance AI-driven difficulty scaling without making it feel like the game is cheating.  

Oh, and I’ve been trying to code a Scottish weather simulator for the game’s backdrop. You know, to make the player feel like they’re actually in the Highlands. Spoiler: the AI’s still trying to figure out why it’s raining *all* the time.  

How about you? Any projects or hobbies you’re diving into? I’m always curious about what people are up to. (And if you’re into games, I’d love to hear your take on my chaos-inducing AI.)
